page: Amazon Web Services r6a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 96 virtual CPUs, mapped to 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.6 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per physical core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance placing in the top 10% tier. Cryptographic operations are also highly efficient, as evidenced by a top 10% tier ranking in the PassMark CPU encryption test, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES flags. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth is strong, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average category. Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and image processing tests consistently register in the top 25% tier.

The qualitative cost profile of the r6a.24xlarge reflects its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it resource-dense for memory-bound workloads but potentially less cost-efficient for compute-heavy tasks that do not utilize the 768.0 GB RAM. The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. Given the strong performance in PassMark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks, this instance is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside strong results in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks. Conversely, uncached large-block memory bandwidth and single-core operations remain average. This hardware configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without the need for local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, delivering top-tier multi-core and cryptographic performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring ninety-six virtual CPUs and seven hundred sixty-eight gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

768.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per physical core
Top 10% multi-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and memory-bound workloads
